title: Choose the cache pattern by consistency need
description: Match cache-aside, read/write-through, or write-behind to the workload's durability and latency tolerance.
body: Use when introducing a cache in front of a datastore and deciding the read/write flow. Do not pick write-behind for data that must survive a crash — its asynchronous flush window can drop unacknowledged writes. Actions: classify the workload as read-heavy (cache-aside), needing a synchronous abstraction (read-through/write-through), or tolerant of async durability (write-behind), then compare each pattern's consistency against its latency. Validation signals include a measured hit rate and a documented staleness bound per pattern. Risk boundary: write-behind trades durability for throughput — escalate before applying it to order or payment state.
